lines: The physiological sigh. [long pause] A Stanford study in 2023 found this is the single most effective real-time breathing technique for reducing stress — more effective than box breathing, meditation breathing, or mindfulness alone. [long pause] It works by fully inflating your lung's alveoli on the double inhale, then offloading excess CO2 on the long exhale, which directly activates your parasympathetic nervous system.
Two quick inhales through the nose. [long pause] One long exhale through the mouth. [long pause] Six cycles. [long pause] I'll guide you.
Short inhale through the nose... [long pause] [long pause] and a deeper second inhale... [long pause] [long pause]
Now a long, slow exhale through the mouth... [long pause] [long pause] let it all out.
Short inhale... [long pause] [long pause] deeper second inhale... [long pause] [long pause]
Long, slow exhale... [long pause] [long pause] empty your lungs completely.
Short inhale... [long pause] [long pause] deeper inhale on top... [long pause] [long pause]
Exhale slowly... [long pause] [long pause] all the way out.
Short inhale... [long pause] [long pause] second deeper inhale... [long pause] [long pause]
And release... [long pause] [long pause] long exhale through the mouth.
Short inhale... [long pause] [long pause] deep inhale... [long pause] [long pause]
Slow exhale... [long pause] [long pause] let everything go.
Short inhale... [long pause] [long pause] deeper second inhale... [long pause] [long pause]
Long, slow exhale... [long pause] [long pause] all the way out.
Six cycles done. [long pause] Breathe normally. [long pause] Notice the shift.
